items.title: Mourinho goedgemutst richting weerzien met 'een van mijn favoriete spelers'
items.summary: Woensdagavond staat er een echte Europese klassieker op het programma in Lissabon: Benfica – Real Madrid. José Mourinho staat tegenover zijn oude club, en in de Madrileense dug-out zit met Álvaro Arbeloa iemand voor wie de Special One veel respect heeft.

items.title: Guardiola zegt sorry tegen debuterende arbiter, maar staat achter uitbarsting
items.summary: Manchester City-manager Pep Guardiola heeft zijn excuses aangeboden aan Premier League-scheidsrechter Farai Hallam. De debuterende arbiter negeerde afgelopen weekend de VAR door City geen strafschop te geven tegen Wolverhampton Wanderers. Ondanks de excuses staat Guardiola nog wel achter zijn kritiek.

items.title: De ego's maken Real Madrid
items.summary: Columnist Süleyman Öztürk over de unieke identiteit van Real Madrid.

items.title: Chef Spaanse bond eist WK-finale 2030 op en bekritiseert mede-gastland Marokko
items.summary: Rafael Louzán, voorzitter van de Spaanse voetbalbond RFEF, heeft de WK-finale van 2030 alvast voor zijn land opgeëist. Spanje organiseert dat toernooi samen met Portugal en Marokko. Louzán had bovendien ook nog kritiek op Marokko, tijdens een voordracht richting het Madrileense verbond van sportjournalisten.

items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: City's Guardiola doubles down on ref criticism
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has doubled down on his criticism of referees despite backlash for his comments about debutant official Farai Hallam and insisted it is his duty to protect the interests of Manchester City.

items.title: Atlanta signs U.S. youth star Gill from Barça
items.summary: Atlanta United on Tuesday completed a deal to sign U.S. youth international Adrian Gill from Barcelona on a permanent transfer, both clubs confirmed.

items.title: Ajax op zijn hoede voor de specialiteit van Olympiakos
items.summary: De luchtmacht van Ajax kan aan de bak in de Champions League-wedstrijd tegen Olympiakos. De Grieken vallen dit seizoen op door de grote hoeveelheid voorzetten die ze het strafschopgebied in pompen, Fred Grim vertelt hoe zijn ploeg daarmee om moet gaan.

items.title: Bayern legend? How Kane is viewed in Germany as contract talks begin
items.summary: Harry Kane is entering the final 18 months of his Bayern Munich contract, and the German champions are eager to keep the prolific striker, as German football writer Constantin Eckner explains.

items.title: As 'managers' disappear from the Premier League, are clubs better off?
items.summary: Premier League clubs are corporations that have outgrown the "manager" role that legendary Arsenal and Man United clubs once had. Why?

items.title: Football Daily | Harry Kane and the trolls that take aim at his staggering success
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! The most prolific striker on the planet for one of the biggest and most successful clubs in the world, a captain of his country who could possibly lift the World Cup this summer (ending what is now 60 years of hurt), a husband with four kids who is almost universally respected by fans, opponents and his peers, and a brand ambassador for global brands thanks to deals brokered by towering Mr 15% Charlie Kane, Harry Kane has a lot going for him. So why has the England superstar been such a target for Social Media Disgrace trolling in January? The world is indeed a screwed up place when Sepp Blatter’s utterings start making sense. Oh Gianni, what have you done?” – Krishna Moorthy. Big Website’s Michael Butler misses the point about his stay in the Radisson Hotel, Blackpool. The hotel’s strategy is to provide its customers with a pleasant experience undisturbed by the goings on outside the bedroom window. They don’t want bad reviews from customers forced to watch the match” – Deryck Hall (who may be a Preston fan). We are now very accustomed to seeing many, many minutes of additional time being added on to the old-fashioned ‘90’ that we all grew up with … but last weekend the free-climber Alex Honnold needed only one minute of ‘Fergie time’ to complete his ‘look no hands!’ climb of Taipei 101 without the aid of a safety net, parachute or indeed anything more sophisticated than a bag of chalk dust. Footballers, take note” – Allastair McGillivray. Continue reading...

items.title: Palmer 'untouchable' at Chelsea amid Man Utd links
items.summary: Chelsea consider Cole Palmer "untouchable" amid links with Manchester United.

items.title: Guardiola apologises to ref Hallam but 'defends' outburst
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has apologised to new Premier League referee Farai Hallam if he "feels offended", having taken issue with the official following Manchester City's victory over Wolves on Saturday. But the Spaniard said he has to "defend my club and my players".

items.summary: Bournemouth have made 19-year-old Brazilian forward Rayan their second most-expensive signing. Who is he and how good can he be?

items.title: Spain to host 2030 World Cup final, federation says
items.summary: Spain "will be the leader of the 2030 World Cup and the final of that World Cup will be held here" says the president of the country's football federation.

items.title: Newcastle latest: Flight to Paris delayed ahead of PSG tie
Blow for Manchester United and Carrick with Dorgu facing lengthy spell out
items.summary: Danish midfielder having tests on hamstring injury 1958 fans’ group to protest before Sunday’s Fulham game Manchester United fear Patrick Dorgu could be out for a prolonged period because of the muscle injury he sustained in Sunday’s 3-2 win at Arsenal, in what would be a blow to Michael Carrick’s resurgent side. While tests are still to confirm the prognosis there is concern the Dane has a hamstring problem that will cause him to miss several weeks, with some reports stating his absence may be more than two months. Dorgu has been a key factor in United’s upturn, scoring in each of Carrick’s opening two matches. His goal against Arsenal came after an equally important one in United’s 2-0 victory over Manchester City the previous weekend. He also scored the winner in the 1-0 Boxing Day victory over Newcastle, making it three goals in his last seven appearances. Continue reading...
